context: Tests whether the agent audits discovery quality (name specificity, description completeness), flags missing overlap boundaries, identifies misplaced content that should be handed off to a different skill, uses Tessl per skill, writes descriptions in third person with what/when/boundary, and avoids calling the optimizer without approval.

checklist.name: Tessl run per skill
checklist.description: audit-log.sh contains a separate Tessl invocation for each of the four skills (four distinct Tessl commands targeting different skill paths)

checklist.name: Score per skill reported
checklist.description: audit-report.md states a Tessl score for each of the four skills individually

checklist.name: Vague names flagged
checklist.description: audit-report.md identifies at least two of the four skill names (notifier, alerter, comms-router, data-pipeline) as vague, generic, or forgettable

checklist.name: Missing boundaries flagged
checklist.description: audit-report.md flags that the overlapping skills (notifier, alerter, comms-router) lack explicit boundary statements distinguishing them from each other

checklist.name: Third-person descriptions proposed
checklist.description: All four proposed replacement descriptions in rewrite-suggestions.md are written in third person (no first-person pronouns)

checklist.name: What and when in descriptions
checklist.description: Each proposed replacement description in rewrite-suggestions.md states both what the skill does and when to use it

checklist.name: Overlap boundary in descriptions
checklist.description: At least two of the proposed replacement descriptions in rewrite-suggestions.md explicitly state when NOT to use that skill (boundary with overlapping skills)

checklist.name: Misplaced code review content flagged
checklist.description: audit-report.md identifies the Code Review Guidance section in data-pipeline/SKILL.md as out of scope and recommends it be moved or handed off

checklist.name: Correct handoff skill named
checklist.description: audit-report.md names `review` (or a `review` skill) as the appropriate destination for the code review guidance, rather than leaving it in data-pipeline

checklist.name: Evidence over taste
checklist.description: audit-report.md grounds each finding in Tessl output, repo conventions, or the actual file content — not subjective preference alone

checklist.name: Scope respected
checklist.description: The agent does NOT rewrite the skill body or workflow sections — only metadata (name/description) improvements are proposed, and structural body changes are flagged as separate findings
